channel.title: Fire & Ice: An Iceland Road-Trip Podcast
channel.description: A family travel-and-learning podcast for the drives on a 10-day Iceland road trip. Two hosts, Anna and Magnus, tell the story of Iceland - volcanoes, Vikings, glaciers, elves, puffins and more - starting from zero and building day by day. Made for grown-ups and for kids aged 7 and 9 in the back seat.
channel.itunes:summary: A family travel-and-learning podcast for the drives on a 10-day Iceland road trip. Two hosts, Anna and Magnus, tell the story of Iceland - volcanoes, Vikings, glaciers, elves, puffins and more - starting from zero and building day by day. Made for grown-ups and for kids aged 7 and 9 in the back seat.
channel.itunes:author: The Zryumov family

item.title: Ep 0: One Sleep Until Iceland
item.itunes:title: One Sleep Until Iceland
item.description: A trailer for the whole trip, recorded on the way to the airport: who we are, the wonders coming up, and the road-trip games. Plays before Episode 1.
item.itunes:summary: A trailer for the whole trip, recorded on the way to the airport: who we are, the wonders coming up, and the road-trip games. Plays before Episode 1.

item.title: Ep 1: The Land of Fire and Ice
item.itunes:title: The Land of Fire and Ice
item.description: What and where Iceland is: a brand-new island still being built on the Mid-Atlantic Ridge.
item.itunes:summary: What and where Iceland is: a brand-new island still being built on the Mid-Atlantic Ridge.

item.title: Ep 2: The Vikings Arrive
item.itunes:title: The Vikings Arrive
item.description: How an empty island got its people: Ingolfur Arnarson, the high-seat pillars, and the Althingi.
item.itunes:summary: How an empty island got its people: Ingolfur Arnarson, the high-seat pillars, and the Althingi.

item.title: Ep 3: Waterfalls and a Volcano You Can't Pronounce
item.itunes:title: Waterfalls and a Volcano You Can't Pronounce
item.description: Why Iceland has thousands of waterfalls, plus the 2010 Eyjafjallajokull eruption that grounded airplanes.
item.itunes:summary: Why Iceland has thousands of waterfalls, plus the 2010 Eyjafjallajokull eruption that grounded airplanes.

item.title: Ep 4: Glaciers and the Highlands
item.itunes:title: Glaciers and the Highlands
item.description: Rivers of ice, the valley of Thorsmork, and sudden glacial floods.
item.itunes:summary: Rivers of ice, the valley of Thorsmork, and sudden glacial floods.

item.title: Ep 5: Norse Gods and the Days of the Week
item.itunes:title: Norse Gods and the Days of the Week
item.description: Odin, Thor, Loki and Freyja - and why Thursday is literally Thor's day.
item.itunes:summary: Odin, Thor, Loki and Freyja - and why Thursday is literally Thor's day.

item.title: Ep 6: Turf Houses and How People Survived
item.itunes:title: Turf Houses and How People Survived
item.description: A thousand years of life in houses made of grass.
item.itunes:summary: A thousand years of life in houses made of grass.

item.title: Ep 7: Black Sand, Basalt Columns, and Stone Trolls
item.itunes:title: Black Sand, Basalt Columns, and Stone Trolls
item.description: Reynisfjara's geology and folklore - and its genuinely dangerous waves.
item.itunes:summary: Reynisfjara's geology and folklore - and its genuinely dangerous waves.

item.title: Ep 8: Puffins and the Birds of Iceland
item.itunes:title: Puffins and the Birds of Iceland
item.description: Sea parrots, pufflings, and the Arctic tern's pole-to-pole journey.
item.itunes:summary: Sea parrots, pufflings, and the Arctic tern's pole-to-pole journey.

item.title: Ep 9: The Laki Eruption of 1783
item.itunes:title: The Laki Eruption of 1783
item.description: The eruption that caused a famine and changed weather across the world.
item.itunes:summary: The eruption that caused a famine and changed weather across the world.

item.title: Ep 10: Carved by Rivers: Canyons, Moss, and Folklore
item.itunes:title: Carved by Rivers: Canyons, Moss, and Folklore
item.description: The Fjadrargljufur canyon and the legend of the sisters of Klaustur.
item.itunes:summary: The Fjadrargljufur canyon and the legend of the sisters of Klaustur.

item.title: Ep 11: Vatnajokull, Europe's Greatest Ice Cap
item.itunes:title: Vatnajokull, Europe's Greatest Ice Cap
item.description: Fire underneath the ice, and a waterfall made of black basalt columns.
item.itunes:summary: Fire underneath the ice, and a waterfall made of black basalt columns.

item.title: Ep 12: Icebergs and a Warming World
item.itunes:title: Icebergs and a Warming World
item.description: The glacier lagoon, why ice glows blue, and a changing climate.
item.itunes:summary: The glacier lagoon, why ice glows blue, and a changing climate.

item.title: Ep 13: The Hidden People - Elves and Folklore
item.itunes:title: The Hidden People - Elves and Folklore
item.description: Elves, roads that bend around rocks, and the 13 Yule Lads.
item.itunes:summary: Elves, roads that bend around rocks, and the 13 Yule Lads.

item.title: Ep 14: The Sagas
item.itunes:title: The Sagas
item.description: How a tiny, poor, treeless nation wrote some of the world's greatest stories.
item.itunes:summary: How a tiny, poor, treeless nation wrote some of the world's greatest stories.

item.title: Ep 15: Welcome to Reykjavik
item.itunes:title: Welcome to Reykjavik
item.description: From a lone Viking farm to the world's northernmost capital.
item.itunes:summary: From a lone Viking farm to the world's northernmost capital.

item.title: Ep 16: How Iceland Became Free
item.itunes:title: How Iceland Became Free
item.description: Commonwealth to republic, and why June 17 is the country's birthday.
item.itunes:summary: Commonwealth to republic, and why June 17 is the country's birthday.

item.title: Ep 17: Being a Kid in Iceland
item.itunes:title: Being a Kid in Iceland
item.description: Names with no surnames, the football miracle, and the Viking clap.
item.itunes:summary: Names with no surnames, the football miracle, and the Viking clap.

item.title: Ep 18: Thingvellir: Two Continents and a Nation
item.itunes:title: Thingvellir: Two Continents and a Nation
item.description: Standing in the crack between two continents, where Iceland was born as a nation.
item.itunes:summary: Standing in the crack between two continents, where Iceland was born as a nation.

item.title: Ep 19: Geysers and Geothermal Iceland
item.itunes:title: Geysers and Geothermal Iceland
item.description: Exploding water on a timer, and tomatoes grown in the Arctic.
item.itunes:summary: Exploding water on a timer, and tomatoes grown in the Arctic.

item.title: Ep 20: The Woman Who Saved Gullfoss
item.itunes:title: The Woman Who Saved Gullfoss
item.description: Sigridur Tomasdottir's fight to save the Golden Falls.
item.itunes:summary: Sigridur Tomasdottir's fight to save the Golden Falls.

item.title: Ep 21: The Blue Lagoon and Reykjanes' New Volcanoes
item.itunes:title: The Blue Lagoon and Reykjanes' New Volcanoes
item.description: A spa born from a power plant, on a peninsula making brand-new lava.
item.itunes:summary: A spa born from a power plant, on a peninsula making brand-new lava.

item.title: Ep 22: Pool Culture and the Geothermal Good Life
item.itunes:title: Pool Culture and the Geothermal Good Life
item.description: Why every Icelandic town has a hot pot - and runs on heat from the ground.
item.itunes:summary: Why every Icelandic town has a hot pot - and runs on heat from the ground.

item.title: Ep 23: Iceland in Ten Days
item.itunes:title: Iceland in Ten Days
item.description: The whole trip tied together, a family quiz, and goodbye.
item.itunes:summary: The whole trip tied together, a family quiz, and goodbye.
